tutorials.title: Tutorials
tutorials.heading: Mrs. Pokebattler is here to guide you through using the Pokebattler Raid Party App
tutorials.description: My husband sometimes forgets to write instructions on how to use his tools. I always have to remind him that people need a bit of guidance some times! Here are some guides for the more complicated parts of the Pokebattler Raid Party app!
tutorials.caution: These tutorials are heavy on the visuals and are a few megabytes in size, consider loading on wifi.

superMega.title: Super Mega Raids
superMega.subtitle: Shields, enrage, and why you MUST bring a Mega
superMega.heading: TLDR: Mrs. Pokebattler explains the shield wall — only true Megas can break it
superMega.body1: Oh my, my husband has really outdone himself this time! Super Mega raids are his newest, nastiest creation — the strongest Mega Legendaries hiding behind a whole wall of shields. Let me walk you through it before your raid group gets caught by surprise.
superMega.body2: Partway through the battle the boss gets ANGRY. It enrages, its defenses shoot up, and a set of shields goes up around it. While even ONE shield is standing, the boss is completely invulnerable — you can whack it all day long and it won't take a scratch.
superMega.body3: Here's the secret: only a TRUE Mega Evolution's charge move can break a shield. Primal Groudon and Primal Kyogre may look the part, but Primals can NOT break shields — and neither can regular Pokémon. So every trainer should put a Mega in their LAST party slot: the moment the boss enrages, your Mega jumps in automatically with enough energy to fire a charge move and smash one shield.
superMega.body4: The biggest bosses — the mythical ones — raise about a dozen shields, so it takes a full lobby of roughly 12 or more trainers, EACH bringing their own Mega, to tear the wall down. Everyone breaks one, and down they all come!
superMega.body5: The rest of your party can be your usual best attackers — they don't need to be Megas, they just can't break shields. Once the shields fall the boss calms back down and it's a normal (well, very BIG) raid. If you use the app's auto battle party it will tuck a Mega into your last slot for you. Good luck out there, dears!
